message:
style: apply rustfmt formatting across atc, server lib, and pane_identity

Run rustfmt on three files that had accumulated formatting drift:

- atc.rs: Reformat long lines throughout DecisionCore, EvidenceLedger,
  AgentRhythm, EProcessMonitor, CalibrationGuard, AtcEngine, SessionSummary,
  and all test modules. Changes include: collapsing short parameter lists
  onto single lines (DecisionCore::new, EProcessMonitor::update), splitting
  long method chains across lines (.loss_matrix.get().copied().unwrap_or),
  reformatting tuple-heavy loss matrix entries in default_liveness_core and
  default_conflict_core onto separate lines, expanding assert! message args
  that exceeded line width, and normalizing match arm formatting in
  SynthesisEvent::timestamp_micros and SessionSummary::absorb.

- lib.rs: Alphabetize module declarations — move `mod ack_ttl` before
  `pub mod atc` to match rustfmt's expected ordering.

- pane_identity.rs: Reformat a chained method call
  (std::env::var("TMUX_PANE").ok().filter(..)) across multiple lines
  to satisfy line-length limits.

All changes are whitespace/formatting only — no logic changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
